---
title: "Client Report - Exploring Names"
subtitle: "Unit 1 Task 1"
author: "Gavin Blair"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html

# Include and execute your code here
df = pl.read_csv("https://posit.byui.edu/content/0d43d66f-d3b7-4c43-8c36-52e3ed69e7f1/names_year_csv.csv")
```

## QUESTION 1

__What was the earliest year that the name ‘Felisha’ was used?__

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
# Q1
df.filter(pl.col('name') == "Felisha")
```


## QUESTION 2

__What year had the most babies named 'David'? How many babies were named 'David' that year?__

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
# Q2
david_ut_max = (
    df.filter(
        pl.col("name") == "David"
    )
    .sort("Total", descending=True)
    .head(1)
    .select(["year", "Total"])
)

print(david_ut_max)

```


## QUESTION 3

__What year did your name hit its peak? How many babies were named your name in that year?__

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
# Q3
gavin_ut_max = (
    df.filter(
        pl.col("name") == "Gavin"
    )
    .sort("Total", descending=True)
    .head(1)
    .select(["year", "Total"])
)

print(gavin_ut_max)
```


## QUESTION 4

__In the most recent year, what was the most common female name in Utah?__ 

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
# Q4
boy_names = ["Liam", "Noah", "Oliver", "James", "Elijah", "William", "Henry", "Lucas", "Benjamin", "Theodore"]

utah_female_max = (
    df.filter(
        pl.col("year").is_between(2018, 2024) &
        pl.col("name").is_in(boy_names).not_()
    )
    .sort(["year", "UT"], descending=[False, True])
    .group_by("year").head(1)
    .select(["year","name", "UT"])
    .sort("year")
)

print(utah_female_max)

```


---
